title: Air-fryer boneless chicken thighs
subtitle: Juicy thighs with crisp edges in under 20 minutes
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs seasoned with smoked paprika, garlic powder, and black pepper, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 18 minutes. The hot circulating air crisps the outside while keeping the inside moist. No browning step, no basting, no oil beyond a light spray.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer chicken thighs became one of the most-searched air-fryer recipes in Britain shortly after the appliance went mainstream in the early 2020s. Boneless thighs are preferred over breast because they stay moist under the high dry heat and are more forgiving of a couple of extra minutes in the basket.

content.text: Pat the thighs dry before seasoning. Moisture on the surface steams the chicken rather than crisping it, and the air fryer's hot circulating air needs a dry surface to produce colour. Ten seconds with kitchen paper makes a visible difference to the finished result.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Pat the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}} dry with kitchen paper. Toss with the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{onion-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} until evenly coated. Place in a single layer in the basket; do not overlap. Cook at 200°C for 18 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the chicken is cooked through with no pink visible and the juices run clear. An internal temperature of 74°C confirms it is done. Rest for 3 minutes before serving.

items.fix: Cook for an additional 3 to 5 minutes. Always take chicken to room temperature for 10 minutes before cooking and check with a thermometer: 74°C internal is the target
items.cause: Thighs were very thick or straight from the fridge
items.symptom: Outside crisp but centre still pink after 18 minutes

items.fix: Use a light coating of oil and do not overcook. Boneless thighs are more forgiving than breast but they will dry out past 80°C internal
items.cause: Skin-off thighs exposed to the high heat without enough fat
items.symptom: Chicken dry on the outside, tough inside

items.fix: Use thighs of similar size and cook in a single layer. If cooking for more than 4, cook in two batches
items.cause: Thighs were not a uniform size, or the basket was overcrowded
items.symptom: Uneven cooking: some pieces overdone while others still raw

content.text: Air-fryer boneless thighs are the fastest route to a weeknight protein. Serve with rice and a green vegetable, slice over a salad, or use as the base for wraps and grain bowls. The spice mix here is a dry rub: it produces a slightly smoky flavour without marinating time. Double the batch and keep in the fridge for meal prep.
